title: pellionia radicans var. grandis
intro: Pellionia radicans var. grandis, also known as the creeping pellionia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Pellionia radicans var. grandis include treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as reducing fever and alleviating pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
